function tighten_permissions() {
  # Get the current user and home directory
  CURRENT_USER=$(whoami)
  HOME_DIR=$(getent passwd "$CURRENT_USER" | cut -d: -f6)

  # Array of dot-files to check for tightening permissions
  DOT_FILES=(".bashrc" ".bash_profile" ".bash_login" ".profile")

  # Loop through each dot-file and tighten permissions if necessary
  for FILE in "${DOT_FILES[@]}"; do
    # Get the current file's permissions
    CURRENT_PERMISSIONS=$(stat -c "%a" "$HOME_DIR/$FILE")

    # Check if the current permissions are more permissive than owner read/write/execute plus group read only (octal 0740)
    if [[ $CURRENT_PERMISSIONS != "0740" ]]; then
      # Tighten permissions to owner read/write/execute plus group read only (octal 0740)
      chmod 0740 "$HOME_DIR/$FILE"
    fi
  done
}

# Call the tighten_permissions function for all interactive users
for USER in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
  # Check if the user has a home directory and is not root
  if [[ -n "$(getent passwd "$USER" | grep -v "root")" ]]; then
    # Call the tighten_permissions function for the current user
    tighten_permissions "$USER"
  fi
done

# Trigger low-space notification if threshold is met or exceeded
